statement: Miðflokkurinn (Centre Party), founded in 2017 by Sigmundur Davíð Gunnlaugsson, is affiliated with the European Conservatives and Reformists (ECR) group at the European level, which includes parties such as the Italian Fratelli d'Italia (led by PM Giorgia Meloni), the Polish Law and Justice party (PiS), and the Sweden Democrats. The party has positioned itself as Eurosceptic and sovereigntist. Reports in Icelandic media have noted contacts between Miðflokkurinn representatives and US Republican Party circles, though the nature and extent of these contacts are not fully documented in public records. Sigmundur Davíð has characterised the government's EU referendum as 'Evrópubrölt' (roughly: 'EU agitation/hustle') and accused the government of concealing its pro-EU agenda from the public and from Althingi.
source_name: Miðflokkurinn — Party website and statements; ECR Party membership; Icelandic media reports
caveats: Political party connections across borders are normal in democratic politics — most Icelandic parties have international affiliations. The characterisation of contacts with the US Republican Party comes primarily from political opponents and media reports, and the significance of any such contacts is disputed. Sigmundur Davíð's 'Evrópubrölt' characterisation is a political opinion, not a factual claim.

related_entries.statement: Miðflokkurinn (Centre Party) holds 8 Althingi seats in session 157. The party was founded in 2017 by Sigmundur Davíð Gunnlaugsson, former Prime Minister (2013–2016), who remains a sitting MP for Norðausturkjördæmi. The party takes a strong anti-EU position, opposing both EU membership and further EEA integration. Current MPs: Bergþór Ólason (Suðvesturkjördæmi), Sigmundur Davíð Gunnlaugsson (Norðausturkjördæmi), Sigríður Á. Andersen (Reykjavíkurkjördæmi norður), Karl Gauti Hjaltason (Suðurkjördæmi), Nanna Margrét Gunnlaugsdóttir (Suðvesturkjördæmi), Ingibjörg Davíðsdóttir (Norðvesturkjördæmi), Snorri Másson (Reykjavíkurkjördæmi suður), Þorgrímur Sigmundsson (Norðausturkjördæmi).

As of March 2026, the main opposition parties' positions on the EU referendum are: Sjálfstæðisflokkurinn (Independence Party, 13 seats) opposes EU membership but has not called for boycotting the referendum. The party's official position emphasises sovereignty, control of natural resources (especially fisheries), and the adequacy of the EEA arrangement. Sjálfstæðisflokkurinn has criticised the 29 August referendum date as unsuitable, arguing meaningful public debate cannot occur during summer holidays. The party's former leader submitted parliamentary resolutions six times to withdraw Iceland's EU application entirely. Miðflokkurinn (Centre Party, 8 seats) is firmly opposed to EU membership and has been the most vocally anti-EU party in Alþingi, consistently arguing against both membership and the referendum process. Framsóknarflokkurinn (Progressive Party, 4 seats) is internally divided but historically EU-sceptic; approximately half of its voters oppose continuing negotiations.

Miðflokkurinn (Centre Party), led by Sigmundur Davíð Gunnlaugsson, is aligned with the European Conservatives and Reformists (ECR) grouping and is strongly opposed to EU integration. In the context of the 2026 EU referendum campaign, Norwegian outlet Altinget.no reported that Miðflokkurinn representatives had met with US Republican Party contacts, and that a political advisor linked to Nigel Farage visited Iceland twice in early 2026. Heimildin also reported on these international connections. Sigmundur Davíð has cultivated ties with international conservative and Eurosceptic movements since founding the party in 2017 after splitting from the Progressive Party. The party holds 8 seats in Althingi as of session 157.

citations.report_title: Bandaríkjamönnum hugnast ekki innganga Íslands í ESB

claims.claim_text: Miðflokkurinn hafi verið á fundum með Repúblikanaflokki Bandaríkjanna.
Norska stjórnmálafréttasíðan Altinget.no birti grein um andstöðu Bandaríkjanna við ESB-aðild Íslands þann 9. mars 2026.

citations.report_title: Trump sagður beita sér í ESB-umræðunni hér á landi

claims.claim_text: Ráðgjafi Nigels Farage hefur rætt við íslenska stjórnmálamenn í Miðflokknum og Sjálfstæðisflokknum.
